body.p: Network interface controller (NIC) teaming is a method to combine or aggregate physical and virtual network interfaces to provide a logical interface with higher throughput or redundancy. NIC teaming uses a small kernel module to implement fast handling of packet flows and a user-space service for other tasks. This way, NIC teaming is an easily extensible and scalable solution for load-balancing and redundancy requirements.
Red Hat Enterprise Linux provides administrators different options to configure team devices. For example:

li.p: Use nmcli to configure teams connections using the command line.
Use the RHEL web console to configure team connections using a web browser.
Use the nm-connection-editor application to configure team connections in a graphical interface.

note.p: NIC teaming is deprecated in Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9. Consider using the network bonding driver as an alternative. For details, see Configuring a network bond.
